name: Academic Enrichment Programs
summary: Academic Engagement Program fosters growth by giving students the opportunity to engage with one another and faculty in research and innovation programs, summer session initiatives, and living-learning communities.

name: Campus Departments
summary: Member organizations of the Campus Departments umbrella in Engage are all those offices, departments, and programs utilizing Engage to provide services and opportunities for students, faculty, staff, and community at GT.

name: Special Scholarships
summary: Scholar Programs (OSP) seeks to recruit the nation’s best student scholars to Georgia Tech to further enrich the composition of the student body, improve the campus, and impact their environment within a global context as students and as alumni.

name: Buzz Studios Filmmaking Club
summary: Buzz Studios is Georgia Tech's premier, award-winning, and longest-running filmmaking club helping to develop student shorts from script to screen since 2001.

name: Student Center Programs Council
summary: The Student Center Programs Council is a student-led programming community that brings more than 50 exciting programs to campus each year. Some of our programs include Sting Break, GT Night at the Aquarium, Homecoming Week, and Midnight Breakfast.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
summary: Alpha Gamma Delta is a group of passionate and involved women who aim to positively contribute to Tech's community through philanthropy, leadership, inclusivity, and personal growth.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi (AKΨ) is Georgia Tech's Premier Co-Ed Professional Fraternity. With a group of 60+ student members, we work to bring together Tech's highest achieving, business-minded students to foster a community of growth and success.

name: Cycling
summary: Georgia Tech Cycling is the premier road and mountain biking organization on campus. We compete in every cycling discipline including mtb, road, CX and track and are open to riders of all ability. Vist https://gtcycling.com/join/ for more info.

name: RoboJackets
summary: RoboJackets is a student-run organization dedicated to the promotion, education, and advancement of the field of robotics through competition and outreach initiatives. Our five competition teams combine the talents of students from a spectrum of majors.

name: Acoustical Society of America
summary: The purpose of the Georgia Tech Chapter of the Acoustical Society of America is to increase and diffuse the knowledge of acoustics and promote its practical applications.

name: Parents Board
summary: The Georgia Tech Parents Board works to actively advance the mission of Parent & Family Programs, the Division of Student Life and the Institute. Board members attend two meetings per year (early fall and late spring) and serve as special advisors to J...

name: T-Book
summary: The Georgia Tech T-Book was originally published by the Georgia Tech YMCA with the intention of providing a guide to the new Tech students about traditions, campus life and history. Today, the T-Book is a Student Publication handed out at convocation.

name: American Nuclear Society
summary: The Georgia Tech student chapter of the American Nuclear Society supports the mission and vision of ANS and hosts a variety of activities, guest speakers, tour trips, and outreach events to increase general understanding of nuclear-related industries.

name: Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity Inc.
summary: Sigma Beta Rho has grown considerably since its inception. It has expanded from its roots on the East Coast to more than forty recognized chapters and colonies that span the breadth of the nation. This growth stems from a reputation for excellent, valu...

name: Student Construction Association
summary: Student Construction Association (SCA) brings students across the campus dedicated to the construction industry.

name: Parent Assistant Leaders at Georgia Tech (PALs)
summary: Parent & Family Programs created a new student leadership position in 2014 called Parent Assistant Leaders (PALs).  This leadership position assists current and prospective parents and family members as they become part of the Georgia Tech Family.  PAL...

name: African American Student Union
summary: The African American Student Union is the oldest and largest Black student organization on campus. Founded in 1968 after the assassination of the late Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., AASU has worked to prove a social and cultural outlet for 54 years

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: Delta Sigma Pi is a professional fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
summary: We are the Alpha Omega Chapter of Alpha Chi Sigma, Georgia Tech's professional co-ed chemistry fraternity. Our organization strives to build its members into better professionals who will augment their respective fields within the chemical sciences.

name: Musician's Network
summary: Musician's Network is the organization that runs Under the Couch (currently displaced on campus). We offer a number of services to musicians and music-lovers, such as concerts, practice spaces, lessons, equipment rentals, and semesterly music festivals.

name: TEAM Buzz
summary:
TEAM Buzz connects Georgia Tech to the local Atlanta community through community service. We encourage student organizations, peer groups, and individuals to join the rest of the GT community to serve together! 

Join as a member to stay updated!

name: Student Polymer Network
summary:
The Student Polymer Network aims to enrich the experience of students interested in polymer sciences & engineering through social, outreach, and professional development events.

Join us at spn@groups.gatech.edu

name: Bridge Club
summary: The Georgia Tech Bridge Club teaches, plays, and promotes the card game of bridge.

name: Executive Round Table
summary: Executive Round Table was founded in 1956 as an organization to promote the entrepreneurial spirit and develop outstanding leadership and character among its members.

name: Startup Exchange
summary: Startup Exchange at Georgia Tech is the place for builders at Georgia Tech to build and launch their most ambitious ideas.

name: Men's Water Polo Club
summary: Georgia Tech Water Polo Club is a competitive club water polo team that plays in the Southeast Division of the Collegiate Water Polo Association (CWPA). The official CWPA season is in the fall; however, we compete at other tournaments in the spring too.

name: Delta Tau Delta
summary: Delta Tau Delta's motto, "Lives of Excellence", is the foundation on which we base our values of brotherhood, leadership, academics, and service. Our Chapter's legacy is evident by the connections we have with our many successful alumni, and involvement.

name: Ramblin' Reck Club
summary: Ramblin' Reck Club is an organization committed to the education and promotion of Tech spirit, history and tradition as well as the caretaking of the Ramblin' Reck.

name: Band Club
summary: The Georgia Tech Band Club performs the administrative and fundraising duties for the various bands on campus.

name: Pride Alliance
summary: Pride Alliance is a Georgia Tech student organization for LGBTQIA students and their allies. We host 5 types of events: social, volunteering, education, professional development, and activism! Reach out if you have any questions or suggestions! :-)

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: ASCE is an organization open to all interested in Civil and Environmental Engineering. Students in ASCE have the opportunity to learn more about the stated industry, and make connections with students who have similar interests.

name: Graduate Student Government Association
summary: The purpose of the Graduate Student Government Association is to enhance the quality of student life at Georgia Tech by providing student participation in the governance of the Institute. We are happy that you are here and hope that you are able to use...

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: SHPE empowers students, of all races and majors, to develop professionally and academically in addition to maintaining the social element of Hispanic culture. We offer our members countless opportunities to develop as students, professionals, and le.

name: Anime O-Tekku
summary: "There's always time to study later." - Official Club Motto. Anime O-Tekku has been Georgia Tech's anime club since 1995. A group of fans put together a student organization that provided a venue in which to watch anime from Japan each weekend...

name: Women in ECE
summary: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering (WECE) was established in Fall 2004 by a group of five women, consisting of both undergraduate and graduate students. The  objective of WECE is to create a community within the ECE department at Georgia Tech...

name: Yellow Jacket Flying Club
summary: The Yellow Jacket Flying Club is an aviation oriented organization at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, GA. The YJFC was founded by several GT students who wanted to fly in 1945, and has a rich history of promoting aviation in the Georgia Tech community.

name: Fulbright Student Association
summary: Group for all Georgia Tech Fulbright students and alumni. Be part of the student organization and live the Fulbright spirit at Tech!

name: Student Organization Finance Office
summary: The Student Organizations Finance Office(SOFO) provides financial support to ensure the success of Georgia Tech’s student-led organizations.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biology Honors Society
summary: Beta Beta Beta, or commonly known as Tri-Beta, is a National Biological Honor Society. The purpose of our society is to recognize achievement, promote scholarship and research, and expand knowledge of the biological sciences.

name: Graduate Association of Physicists
summary: The Graduate Association of Physicists (GAP) is a graduate student organization in the Georgia Tech School of Physics that aims to provide mentoring, networking and career development opportunities to physics PhD students.

name: Yellow Jacket Space Program
summary:
Georgia Tech's Space Program

https://www.gtspaceprogram.com/

name: Asian American Student Association
summary: The Asian American Student Association at GT is a cultural organization seeking to promote diversity and provide a community for Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) students on campus. We hope to expand the definition of being an Asian American

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
summary: As one of Georgia Tech’s largest social fraternities, Tau Kappa Epsilon exemplifies leadership, character, and diversity while fostering a healthy environment focused on brotherhood, campus involvement, and personal growth.

name: Men's Ultimate Frisbee
summary: Men's Ultimate Frisbee is a close-knit, competitive team that competes at a national level. We practice throughout the year and travel across the Southeast to compete against other universities.  We have an A and B team to best serve players' goals and...

name: Chamber Choir
summary: The Georgia Tech Chamber Choir is Tech's select, mixed vocal ensemble that performs challenging literature in multiple concerts each year under the direction of Dr. Joshua Palkki. Chamber Choir tours yearly, and hosts a student-run concert each semester.

name: Meditation Club
summary: The Meditation Club provides a time and place for student well-being through the practice of meditation. Join us for weekly in person and virtual meditation sessions!

name: The Tower Undergraduate Research Journal
summary: The Tower is Georgia Tech's undergraduate research journal. We publish undergraduate student submissions on individual and team research projects. reviews of recent research literature and opinion pieces related to research, among others.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
summary: The student chapter of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME) at the Georgia Institute of Technology is a dedicated student-run organization which strives to provide professional development opportunities for mechanical engineering students.

name: Taiwanese Student Association
summary: The Taiwanese Student Association (TSA) at Georgia Tech is a non-profit student organization that focuses on enriching the college experience of both undergraduate and graduate Taiwanese students who share interest and background in Taiwanese culture.

name: Junior STEM
summary: Junior STEM is an organization that aims to encourage and facilitate student involvement in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math (STEM) initiatives to promote and increase the retention of underrepresented students and minorities in STEM careers

name: Trailblazers
summary: Trailblazers is an official student organization of the Georgia Institute of Technology. The purpose of Trailblazers is to increase student and faculty appreciation for the outdoors through outdoor service and recreation.

name: Phi Sigma Kappa
summary: Phi Sigma Kappa or Phi Sig is a social fraternity within the Interfraternity Council. We strive to live out our Cardinal Principles of Brotherhood, Scholarship, and Character in our everyday lives.

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
summary: The Alpha-Tau chapter of Kappa Sigma has been at Georgia Tech for more than 125 years, and has continuously served and bettered the Institute's community. Our brotherhood prides itself on being strong, well-rounded, ambitious, and involved in every par.

name: Taal Tadka
summary: Taal Tadka is GT's 1st and only South Asian fusion a cappella group! We perform medleys of songs in Hindi, English, and other languages. As our name suggests, we believe in creating a "spiced-up rhythm" through just our voices to encompass all cultures!

name: Phi Gamma Delta
summary: We are an IFC fraternity at Georgia Tech. On campus, we pride ourselves on academics, athletics, and our commitment to serve Georgia Tech and the community as a whole.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) is the honor society of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE). Georgia Tech's Beta Mu chapter seeks to recognize and encourage outstanding juniors and seniors in the School of ECE and College of Computing.

name: North Avenue Review
summary: North Avenue Review (NAR) is Tech's free speech magazine. Established in 1989, we launched in response to student protests against Tiananmen Square. To this day, NAR provides all students and faculty with a platform to share thoughts, opinions, and more.

name: RoboGrads
summary: Robotics Graduate Student Organization.

name: Korean Student Association
summary: KSA is the official Korean Student Association at Georgia Tech. We serve as a community for graduate students and postdoctoral researchers with Korean heritage. The total number of graduate student members exceeds about four hundred.

name: Korean Undergraduate Student Association
summary: Started in the early 1990s, the Georgia Tech Korean Undergraduate Student Association (GT KUSA) is the forefront Korean cultural organization on campus. GT KUSA offers numerous services including athletics, academic support, and networking.

name: Pakistani Student Association
summary: PSA is a non-profit cultural organization that serves as platform to bring together Pakistani students and promote Pakistani culture at Georgia Tech. We hold events and mixers, on a regular basis, to develop a well-knit Pakistani Student Community and ...

name: Kappa Kappa Psi: Iota
summary: Kappa Kappa Psi operates primarily as a student service and leadership recognition society whose chief aim is to assist the Director of Bands in developing the leadership and enthusiasm that is required of the Georgia Tech Bands.

name: PianoForte
summary: PianoForte (the Student Pianist Organization) is a student-run organization that was re-established in the Fall, 2018. It creates a network for solo pianists on campus and works towards increasing accessible practice opportunities for its members. Our...

name: Erato
summary: Erato is Georgia Tech's Literature and Arts magazine. Students are responsible for designing and publishing a journal each spring that showcases various types of writing and artwork submitted by members of the GT community.

name: China Care Club
summary: We are a student organization dedicated to raising funds for vulnerable children in Asia. We also volunteer with local adopted Chinese children to help them connect with their Chinese heritage through monthly Playgroups!

name: Runnin' Wreck
summary: Runnin' Wreck is Georgia Tech's running club. We provide a friendly running community to runners of all ability levels, with both easy runs and workouts in Atlanta, and races through NIRCA on some weekends (there are long runs on many others as well).

name: Association of Chemical Engineering Graduate Students
summary: Our organization serves the graduate student community in the department of Chemical & Biomolecular Engineering at Georgia Tech. We host events with the goal of enhancing the overall graduate school experience academically, professionally and socially.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Association
summary: The purpose of MEGA is to support and develop the Mechanical Engineering Graduate Students academically, professionally, and socially.  MEGA serves as a liaison between faculty, administrators, and students.

name: Iranian Student Association
summary: The Iranian Student Association (ISA) is an official student organization under Georgia Institute of Technology, aiming to promote the Iranian culture amongst both Iranian and non-Iranian individuals at Georgia Tech and the Atlanta area.

name: Feminist Leaders Advocating and Mobilizing for Equity
summary: FLAME is a student-chartered organization focused on planning events for and providing resources to GT feminist leaders, working closely with and supporting the mission and goals of the Women's Resource Center.

name: Office of Student Integrity
summary: The Office of Student Integrity inspires students to strengthen their personal character by promoting and upholding Georgia Tech's core value of integrity.

name: Blacks in Business at Georgia Tech
summary: The mission of Blacks in Business is to unite Scheller students in a culture of professionalism for the purpose of accelerating development in various industries while building a sustainable community of support that empowers its members. Through...

name: Invention Studio
summary: The Invention Studio is the largest student-run makerspace in the nation. It is free to use and open to all in the Georgia Tech community. We provide access to many tools, such as 3D printers, laser cutters, waterjets, wood tools, and more.

name: ANAK Society
summary: ANAK's purpose is to honor outstanding juniors and seniors who have shown both exemplary leadership and a true love for Georgia Tech. ANAK was founded in 1908 and is unique to the Institute.

name: Bioengineering Graduate Association
summary: The role of the BGA is to provide input to the Program Chair relating to the broad issues that affect graduate students in the Interdisciplinary Bioengineering Graduate Program as well as to matters that have the potential to enhance student experiences

name: Sigma Chi
summary: SIGMA CHI FRATERNITY HISTORYThe Sigma Chi Fraternity was founded at Miami University of Ohio on July 28th, 1855. Today, it is one of the oldest, largest, and most prestigious collegiate fraternities in nation. The fundamental purpose of Sigma Chi is th...

name: Stamps Health Services Ambassadors
summary: Stamps Health Services Ambassadors is a unique student-led organization that works closely with the healthcare providers at Stamps Health Services. Our purpose is to provide Stamps Health Services with volunteer services from GT pre-health students...

name: BAPS Campus Fellowship
summary: The BAPS Campus Fellowship purpose is to unite students on Georgia Tech’s campus and provide opportunities to learn about Hindu heritage and culture and a more Swaminarayan way of life.

name: Gymnastics Club
summary: We are Georgia Tech's Club Gymnastics Team! No experience is required, so ANYONE can join! We compete both locally and nationally with the National Association of Intercollegiate Gymnastics Clubs (NAIGC).

name: Electrical and Computer Engineering Ambassadors
summary: ECE Ambassadors strives to increase ECE student involvement in admissions events, community outreach, and interaction with faculty, and to encourage the development of future Georgia Tech electrical and computer engineers.

name: PhD 2 Consulting Club
summary: The Georgia Tech PhD2Consulting Club is a graduate student organization comprised of PhD students from a diverse set of departmental backgrounds. Our mission is to inform graduate students of opportunities within the management consulting industry, hel.

name: Starter Bikes
summary: We are a bike co-op. We will help you fix your bike. We will also sell you a bike. We have tools, parts and knowledge available. Come see us at Starter Bikes, located in the bottom of the CRC parking deck, on Fridays from 4-7 PM (last Fridays 4-6 PM).

name: Alpha Phi Alpha
summary: The Nu Mu Chapter of Alpha Phi Alpha was founded at the Southern Institute of Technology, now known as Southern Polytechnic State University. The Nu Mu Chapter was founded on the same principles as Alpha Phi Alpha, manly deeds, scholarship, and love fo...

name: MBA Veterans Club
summary: The MBA Veterans Club is committed to helping active duty and veteran military members advance their careers through a world class education from the Scheller MBA Program.

name: Student Planning Association
summary: SPA is a student organization within Georgia Tech's School of City and Regional Planning that advocates for students in SCaRP and organizes a variety of social, academic, professional, and volunteer events.

name: Georgia Tech Arts
summary: Georgia Tech Arts weaves art into the learning and research of students and faculty across the Institute through a blend of professional performances, exhibitions, installations, residencies, and support for student & faculty ideas.

name: Briaerean Honor Society
summary: The Briaerean Honor Society (BHS) is Georgia Tech's co-op and internship honor society. BHS is the oldest honor society on Tech's campus, present since 1922, and recognizes co-op and intern students who possess an exemplary scholastic record.

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
summary: We are the Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society! Our club is a collection of undergraduates of all majors bonded together by a love of science. We do a variety of programs throughout the year, including K-12 volunteering, trivia, and more!

name: Equity in Architecture
summary: Equity in Architecture is an organization within the School of Architecture whose main goal is to promote unity and equity in our profession through three central points: inclusion, identity, and power.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
summary: Welcome to the Georgia Tech chapter of the Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers! We are the premier student organization that provides Industrial Engineering students with professional, technical, and social opportunities.

name: First-Year Leadership Initiative
summary: Freshmen Leadership Initiative (FLI) is the Georgia Tech Student Foundation (GTSF)'s first-year leadership organization (FLO). FLI members learn and apply the Student Foundation's tenets of philanthropy, community and professional preparedness.

name: Indonesian Student Association
summary: We are a cultural non-profit organization aimed at promoting Indonesian culture in Georgia Tech and the Atlanta community, as well as providing support for all Indonesian students in Georgia Tech.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
summary: A Student Chapter of NOMA. We the students of the Georgia Tech Chapter of NOMAS have come together to enhance our education, network with professionals, and begin to define solutions that will enhance the architectural world.

name: Residence Hall Association
summary: RHA is the second largest student organization at Tech, with nearly 9,000 on-campus residents and members and an annual budget of over $250,000. Join RHA to engage in leadership, community building, event planning, advocacy, and more!

name: CRU
summary: We are a caring and supportive community passionate about connecting students to Jesus. Our vision is to develop spiritual leaders to truly know and follow Jesus Christ at Georgia Tech, in Atlanta, and throughout the world.

name: Board Game Club
summary: Georgia Tech's premiere board gaming club. We have regular open board gaming sessions in the student center.

name: Energy Club
summary: The Energy Club is a professional club dedicated to promoting energy education, innovation, and sustainability. We aim to advance discussion about pressing energy challenges, engage diverse sectors to impact the energy industry, and foster a passion for

name: Women's Water Polo
summary: The Georgia Tech Women's Water Polo team competes against other college club teams within the Southeast Division of the CWPA (Collegiate Water Polo Association) and is always welcoming new players, regardless of experience!

name: Chi Phi
summary: The Chi Phi Fraternity found its origin in 1824, making it 200 years old. Having brothers hailing from Georgia, the US, and the world. Here you will meet people from all walks of life who embody our core values of Truth, Honor, and Personal Integrity

name: Student Hospital Connections
summary: SHC is a pre-health organization focused on providing Atlanta-based clinical opportunities for students interested in the healthcare field. We aim to remove some of the financial burden associated with traveling to clinical opportunities within Atlanta.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: The Society of Physics Students is a social and academic club that invites any Georgia Tech student with an interest in physics, mathematics, or science in general. Being a Physics major is not a requirement to join our organization!

name: College Republicans
summary:
We're a conservative group at Georgia Tech, committed to putting America first. Join us weekly for discussions and guest speakers. 

Get the latest updates here: https://groupme.com/join_group/67101174/9XSXzsGG

name: New Student and Transition Programs
summary: The Office of New Student and Transition Programs supports new and continuing student success in the Georgia Tech community through a wide range of programs.

name: Materials Research Society
summary: MRS is a national organization devoted to promoting and advancing materials science. As stated by the national organization, "the Materials Research Society (MRS) is an organization of materials researchers worldwide that promotes communication for the...

name: Student Neuroscience Association
summary: The Student Neuroscience Association at GT is dedicated to fostering student interest in neuroscience through volunteering, dissections, visits to local neuro-related research centers, speaker events, and social events.

name: American Institute of Architecture Students
summary: The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is an independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to providing programs, information, and resources on issues critical to architectural education and the architectural profession.

name: Graduate Business Council
summary: The purpose of the GBC is to: A. Serve as the student government organization for the full-time MBA program at Georgia Tech. B. To promote social and professional interaction among current students, alumni, faculty, staff, and all other potential stakeh.

name: Undergraduate Consulting Club
summary: The Undergraduate Consulting Club is the oldest & most prestigious consulting club on campus. It offers a wide variety of pro-bono projects, networking events, case competitions, and trainings that give educational, hands-on experience to the industry.

name: Astronomy Club
summary: Promoting amateur astronomy on Tech’s campus and its neighborhood. We meet every Monday at 7:30pm in Howey, and go to the GT observatory (weather permitting). We participate in an assortment of other astronomy activities during the semester.

name: Student Engagement & Well-Being
summary: This portal serves the Division of Student Engagement & Well-Beings's internal programs and events. Membership is limited to current professional staff members.

name: Marksmanship Club
summary: The Marksmanship Club provides the opportunity to discuss, learn about and practice marksmanship and firearms. We strongly welcome beginners and masters alike.

name: Society of Plastics Engineers
summary: The Society of Plastics Engineers provides undergraduate and graduate students at GT with an opportunity to participate in a professional society which provides networking opportunities and scientific and engineering knowledge related to polymers

name: Sigma Nu
summary: Sigma Nu identifies itself by providing members with a socially enhanced Georgia Tech experience. As a larger social fraternity, we believe our brothers are able to get the most out of Tech, both socially and academically.

name: Brazilian Student Association
summary: The Brazilian Student Association at Georgia Tech

name: Phi Kappa Tau
summary: We are an IFC social fraternity at Georgia Tech. Phi Kappa Tau prides itself on developing men of character into men of distinction.

name: HyTech Racing
summary: HyTech Racing is an award-winning electric racing team at Georgia Tech. We design, build, and race a new electric vehicle each year and participate in annual FSAE competitions with teams from other prestigious engineering universities around the world.

name: Technical Association of the Pulp and Paper Industry
summary: GT TAPPI is the top talent pool for the forest products industries. Georgia Tech TAPPI connects Georgia Tech scientists and engineers to the forest products, pulp, paper, packaging, and related industries by delivering growth opportunities and profession

name: Society of Women in Business
summary: It is our mission as Society of Women in Business (“SWiB”) to foster the empowerment of women in the workplace through establishment of corporate connections and enhancement of business knowledge.  As a powerful networking organization, SWiB will give ...

name: Ice Hockey Club
summary: Georgia Tech Ice Hockey, founded in 1973, is the longest continuously run college hockey team in the South. Part of the ACCHL, the team plays in 20+ regular-season weekend games all across America from August to February, with playoff games in March.

name: Society of Women in Physics
summary: The Georgia Institute of Technology’s Society of Women in Physics (SWiP) encourages interest and leadership of women in physics. We spread awareness of the achievements and impact of female physicists as well as career opportunities to women of all ages.

name: Psychology Association
summary: Psychology Association at Georgia Tech (ΨA at GT) is an organization open to all majors who are interested in psychology! The Psych Association brings students together through social activities and learning opportunities.

name: Hong Kong Student Association
summary: We aim to unite the Hong Kong population at Georgia Tech, the Cantonese diaspora, and anyone interested in Cantonese culture, language, and issues.

name: Caribbean Students Association
summary: The Caribbean Students Association of Georgia Tech (GT CaribSA) is a center for the facilitation and promotion of Caribbean culture on campus. We seek to achieve the following objectives:1. Provide a welcoming environment for free cultural expression by.

name: International Ambassadors at Georgia Tech
summary: GTIA champions diversity & inclusion on campus through providing support to the current and incoming international student community as well as through vibrant cultural events such as Night Market and Culture Fest that celebrate diversity on campus.

name: Alpha Pi Mu (Industrial Engineering Honor Society)
summary: Our Mission is to prepare our members for professional success, encourage collaboration between students and members of the faculty, and serve the needs of the Industrial Engineering community, while recognizing outstanding academic achievement.

name: Amateur Radio Club
summary: One of the oldest clubs at Tech, the Amateur Radio Club (known by its callsign W4AQL) hosts activities for students interested in radio or looking to make use of what they learn! We host meetings and Open Shacks for anyone to visit in Van Leer E496-A.

name: National Society of Black Engineers at Georgia Tech
summary: The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community. Founded in 1976, the Georgia Tech Society of...

name: GEMS
summary: GEMS is the Georgia Tech Evening MBA Students organization. Our primary goal is to bring the Evening MBA students together to grow, improve, and leave lasting positive impression on the Institute.

name: Tau Beta Pi
summary: The Tau Beta Pi Association (commonly Tau Beta Pi, ΤΒΠ, or TBP) is the oldest engineering honor society in the United States and the second oldest collegiate honor society in America.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association at Geogia Tech
summary: The Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for students of Chinese heritage as well as those with broader Asian cultural backgrounds.

name: Transfer Student Association
summary: Our mission is to help newly acquired or already established transfer students acclimate to the unique atmosphere of Georgia Tech and optimize their path to graduation.

name: Chi Epsilon
summary: Chi Epsilon is an elite Civil and Environmental Engineering Honor Society. It is an invitation-only organization and membership is extended to students who rank in the upper 30% of their respective Junior, Senior, and Graduate class of the department.

name: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc.
summary: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc. is a 501(c)(7) non-profit organization that cultivates individual growth and promotes strong fellowship through various programs. Our national philanthropy is mental health awareness, emphasizing advocacy and awareness in the ...

name: Honor Advisory Council
summary: We are a student-led group open to both undergraduate and graduate students that teaches about Academic Integrity and the Honor Code through fun events including conferences, giveaways, and arts & crafts. We also mentor students through OSI sanctions.

name: Zeta Beta Tau
summary: The brothers of ZBT are successful leaders and gentlemen. Established nationally in 1898 and at Georgia Tech in 1916, our brotherhood is an institution of excellence. We endeavor to be involved philanthropically, academically, and athletically.

name: FirstGen
summary: FirstGen is focused on creating an inclusive community for First-Generation College Students at Tech. We want to foster a sense of pride among us through peer mentoring and fun social events. We also facilitate the Growing Up in Science Speaker Series!

name: Bangladesh Student Association
summary: We are a non-partisan organization aimed at bringing together the Bangladeshi student community at Georgia Tech, promoting Bangladeshi culture, and helping those in need in Bangladesh and elsewhere. Follow us on Instagram to be kept up to date: bsa.gt

name: Samulnori Club at Georgia Tech
summary: We provide Georgia Tech students with opportunity to learn and enjoy playing Samulnori, Korean traditional percussion music.

name: Association of Environmental Engineers and Scientists
summary: AEES is a student-run organization for environmentally-minded engineers and scientists. We connect undergraduate and graduate students with industry, faculty, and fellow peers through regular lunch meetings, volunteering opportunities, and social events.

name: TEDxGeorgiaTech
summary: TEDxGeorgiaTech is an independently organized TEDx event in Atlanta, Georgia. We are proud to be entirely student-run. Our goal is to showcase ideas from our own community that inspire change on Georgia Tech campus and beyond.

name: Arab Student Organization
summary: The Arab Student Organization (ASO) is a cultural non-profit organization focused on uniting the Arab community at Georgia Tech and those interested in Arab culture, tradition, and language.

name: Glee Club
summary: The Georgia Tech Glee Club is a male vocal fach ensemble. It is the oldest student organization on campus, having completed World Tours and several studio albums.

name: Turkish Student Organization
summary: TSO represents Turkish students and Turkish culture at Georgia Tech. We help new students adapt to life in USA and foster intercultural enrichment by offering a glimpse of our culture in various events and activities.

name: Infinite Harmony
summary: Love to sing?  We're Infinite Harmony, Georgia Tech's premier co-ed a cappella group!  WE WILL BE HOLDING AUDITIONS FOR FALL 2025!  If you would like to audition with us, you can fill out our interest form on our website!

name: Nu Chapter of Pi Tau Sigma
summary: PI TAU SIGMA  is the International Honor Society for Mechanical Engineers. Pi Tau Sigma members are chosen on a basis of sound engineering ability, scholarship service, leadership, and integrity, and represent the top 25% of undergrads and 35% of grads.

name: VGDev
summary: No experience? No problem! We're an organization that makes video games; from the engine design all the way to logo graphics! All made by Tech students!

name: Industrial Designers Society of America
summary: Georgia Tech's chapter of the international professional organization for Industrial Designers, IDSA. We strive to enhance the experience of industrial design students and people interested in industrial design alike by hosting career-focused events.

name: Student Competition Center Governing Board
summary: The Student Competition Center Governing Board is a collection of representatives from each student competition team within the Student Competition Center. The Board enables all of its constituent teams to address shared concerns and facility management.

name: College Democrats
summary: College Democrats of Georgia Tech (CDGT) is dedicated to promoting a progressive message on Georgia Tech's campus.

name: Student Alumni Association
summary: Our mission is to enhance the experience of each and every Tech student by providing opportunities that broaden the college experience, strengthen traditions, build lifelong loyalty to Georgia Tech, and prepare every student for success after college.

summary: African Graduate Students Connect (AGSC) exists to provide a unique social, professional and academic network to promote sociocultural enrichment, academic success, and Africa-related volunteering opportunities for graduate students of African descent...

name: Caterpillar's Promise
summary: The purpose of Caterpillar's Promise, Inc. is to motivate others, especially the youth, to use their talents for the greater good through service, seminars, and showcases. We strive to ENCOURAGE, ENLIGHTEN, EXCITE, EDUCATE, and ELEVATE!

summary: .The ABLE Alliance aims to connect students at Georgia Tech and build community while connecting them with career opportunities.

summary: Mock Trial provides an opportunity for students to learn about the American legal system. We field four competitive teams and participate in simulated trials hosted by the American Mock Trial Association. See www.georgiatechmocktrial.com for more info!

name: Civic Engagement

summary: Women in Materials Science and Engineering serves as a community to MSE women students and faculty geared towards their social, professional and career development.

summary: The Georgia Tech John H. Martinson Honors Program (JMHP) fosters curiosity, creativity, and connection. JMHP students are encouraged to pursue their interests across disciplinary boundaries and the boundary between theory and practice.

summary: Grad Pride is a student org run by LGBTQIA grads, post-docs, and allies at Georgia Tech. We meet a couple times a month for social mixers and professional development activities.

summary: Science Olympiad at Georgia Tech is dedicated to engaging Science Olympiad alumni in volunteering at tournaments, and generally improving the state of Science Olympiad.

summary: Ignite your Georgia Tech experience by applying to one of our First Year Summer Launch Programs! All first year summer students enter Georgia Tech for the late summer term and will participate in one of the Georgia Tech Summer Launch Programs. Students.

summary: Serve as a mentor for the bright young middle and high school students who want to learn more about technology and computer science by teaching basic coding lessons. We also provide fun networking and social engagement opportunities to our own members!

summary: The Undergraduate Research Ambassadors are dedicated to fostering collaboration among those in research and increasing access for those interested in getting involved.

summary: GT 1000: First-Year Seminar & GT 2000: Transfer Seminar are one-credit hour courses designed to support the successful transition and experience of incoming students. These courses are letter-graded and offered in fall, spring, and summer semesters.

summary: Our mission is to be the leader in collegiate recreation by providing comprehensive and engaging programs and services that strengthen community wellbeing and enhance student success.

summary: The purpose of the Filipino Student Association at Georgia Tech is to promote Filipino Culture and the education of Filipino traditions among students. The club intends to build relationships between students through social events, meetings, and service

summary: We are an organization that uses simple math, writing and reading aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease. We encourage intergenerational collaboration.

summary: To provide a safe, supportive, and instructive place for anyone and everyone who is interested in learning Kendo, Japanese swordsmanship.

The purpose of Beekeeping Club is to:
1) Care for hives on Georgia Tech’s campus

We have new member dues of $10 a year.

summary: First-Generation Student Programs (FGSP) collaborates with diverse campus and community partners to provide support through co-curricular programming for first-generation students.

summary: A chapter of the Association of Latino Professionals for America (ALPFA), the country's leading professional Latino non-profit organization consisting of over 95,000 members. We are focused on providing professional development opportunities and career

name: AeroAfroAstro
summary: AeroAfroAstro (est. 2022) is a professional aerospace organization created to support Black students & Allies earning careers in the aerospace industry. AeroAfroAstro hosts speaker panels, on-site company tours, community events, and more. Join today!

summary: IASO aims to build community and engagement within the Sam Nunn School of International Affairs student body and faculty, share resources and experiences related to studying International Affairs at Georgia Tech, and promote global awareness on campus.

summary: StORM Club is Georgia Tech’s meteorology hub. We promote community awareness and provide students with fun, productive opportunities to network and jumpstart their atmospheric careers.

summary: The Georgia Tech Real Estate Club provides opportunities for students to learn and engage with Atlanta area Real Estate projects, professionals, and firms through workshops, site tours, and industry presentations to further their career prospects.

summary: Birdwatchers aims to increase appreciation for birds on campus, connect students with other birdwatchers and ornithologists, and provide opportunities for the greater enjoyment of birds.

summary: Ghanaian Student Association @ Georgia Tech exists to bring together all GT students of Ghanaian descent together.

summary: Young Americans for Liberty at Georgia Tech aims to create a campus community that champions individual liberty, limited government, and personal responsibility.

name: SiliconJackets
summary: The main mission of SiliconJackets is to create an environment which makes the design of silicon hardware accessible to students.

summary: The purpose of the Student Chapter is to provide opportunities for the professional development of its members related to the design and construction of concrete structures and activities in the concrete industry.

summary: The principal objectives of the Association shall be to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of all citizens.

summary: The purpose of the organization is to create a platform through which Georgia Tech students of all majors and ages can compete in the US Department of Energy's Solar Decathlon competition on an annual basis.

summary: The National Residence Hall Honorary is a national organization devoted to honoring those who embody its pillars of Service and Recognition and furthering the pursuit of those pillars across the nation.​

summary: GTSC's purpose is to bring the design, analysis, optimization, building, and testing of high-speed aircraft systems into the hands of Georgia Tech students.

summary: The purpose of DRXC is to promote radio-controlled multirotor and fixed-wing design, building, and flight as a hobby among Georgia Tech students and to support radio-controlled aircraft competition teams.

summary: ISPA@GT shares knowledge on opportunities and obstacles for foreign nationals in the aerospace industry and advocates for issues faced by international students studying aerospace engineering in the United States.

summary: Through S&TPC, students have the opportunity to craft innovative policy solutions to prominent science & technology issues and present these policy solutions to state and federal policymakers.

summary: The purpose of WISyE is to advance and support women’s experience, learning, and networking in Industrial and Systems Engineering at Georgia Tech.

summary: The Robotics Outreach Club at Georgia Tech is an organization that encourages and facilitates long-term volunteering with and mentoring local robotics teams.

name: Anesthesiologist Assistant Association @ GT
summary: We aim to help bring awareness to the anesthesiologist assistant profession and help Georgia Tech students aspiring to become AAs build well rounded applications through assisting in getting shadowing hours, writing a personal statement, etc.

name: Women in Construction Chapter @ GT
summary: The Women in Construction Chapter serves to support and empower women working or interested in the construction industry, providing a platform for networking, mentorship, and professional development.

name: Black students in Material Science and Engineering
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create a supportive and empowering community for Black students within the school of Material Science and Engineering (MSE) at Georgia Tech.

name: Create for Kids at Georgia Tech
summary: Create For Kids is a nonprofit organization focused on fostering creativity and inspiring children in hospital settings.

name: Propulsive Landers @ GT
summary: Welcome to our page! We're Georgia Tech's 3rd rocketry team with the mission to become the first student organization in the world to achieve vertical take-off and landing of a hybrid rocket. Join us on our mission to create something extraordinary.

name: Supply Chain and Logistics Organization
summary: To foster the development of supply chain knowledge, networking opportunities, and professional growth for Georgia Tech students through a variety of resources to build a strong community of future supply chain professionals.

name: National Marrow Donor Program @ Georgia Tech
summary: Engage students in the NMDP® mission to save lives by providing meaningful, unique opportunities for students to join the blood stem cell registry and learn about blood cancers and other life-threatening diseases treated by a blood stem cell transplant.

name: Project Yellow
summary: Project Yellow aims to provide mental health resources and education to children and elementary schoolers near Georgia Tech.

name: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society at GT
summary: We provide a means for students at Georgia Tech to meet and fulfill the mission of LLS. a. The official mission of our national affiliate, The Leukemia & Lymphoma Society (LLS) is “to cure leukemia, lymphoma, Hodgkin’s disease and myeloma

name: Ethiopian and Eritrean Graduate Student Association at Georgia Institute of Technology
summary: The purpose of our organization is to connect and provide a support system to new and existing graduate students with Ethiopian and Eritrean ethnic backgrounds at Georgia Tech.

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. Georgia Institute of Technology Chapter
summary: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc. is committed to uphold and enhance the interests and aspirations of Latino Unity for the achievement of social justice for all Latinos.

name: Georgia Tech Library
summary: Georgia Tech Library

name: American Sign Language Club at Georgia Tech
summary: ASL Club aims to share resources for people to learn American Sign Language, forge connections to the Deaf community in the greater Atlanta area, and foster an environment more inclusive to deaf and hard of hearing people.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia Tech
summary: Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia Tech aims to gather a group of Georgia Tech students that are interested in the legal world, law, writing, and editing.

name: Motorcycle Club at Georgia Tech
summary: Our mission is to promote safe riding practices, foster community among motorcycle enthusiasts, and explore the joy of two-wheeled adventure.

name: Fighting Game Community at Georgia Tech
summary: FGC@GT seeks to unite fighting game players and fans in an inclusive community which encourages each other to improve through a casual and competitive environment.

name: The Association of Women in Mathematics Graduate Chapter
summary: We are a graduate chapter of the (national) Association for Women in Math. Our goal is to support female graduate students in their academic careers and to promote math in general.

name: Period Project @ GT
summary: At The Period Project, we’re improving the lives of menstruators one period at a time.

name: The HealthLink Initiative at Georgia Tech
summary: The Health Link Initiative is dedicated to connecting uninsured, undocumented, and underserved communities of Georgia with accessible healthcare services.

name: Central Asian Student Association
summary: The goal of CASA is to create representation of students from Central Asian Countries (Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Turkmenistan).

name: Novel Engineering for Alternative Lift Aerospace
summary: Neal Aerospace aims to research and develop small scale alternative propulsion devices.

name: Black Student-Athletes Association at Georgia Tech
summary: This organization seeks to bridge this gap by fostering a supportive network of black student-athletes while also creating opportunities for greater connection with the entire broader Georgia Tech community.

name: The Neurodiversity Initiative at Georgia Tech
summary: The purpose of the Neurodiversity Initiative is to Volunteer with and provide resources for families of children with special needs in underserved populations in the Atlanta Community, specifically focusing on underserved populations

name: Epsilon Eta
summary: Epsilon Eta is a gender-inclusive environmental fraternity dedicated to fostering a community of passionate environmental changemakers.

name: Public Policy Graduate Student Association at Georgia Tech
summary: PPGSA’s purpose is to connect the Carter School of Public Policy (CSPP) graduate student body population.

name: Students Supporting Israel
summary: The goal of SSI is to educate students of all backgrounds on Israeli history, culture, and current events.

name: Heart of Passion at Georgia Tech
summary: Heart of Passion at Georgia Tech aims to provide emotional support for young adult cancer survivors by facilitating connections with peers and student volunteers.

name: Malayalees @ GT
summary: Malayalees @ GT aims to unite students of Malayalee heritage.

name: PoliScope
summary: PoliScope aims to foster a well-informed and engaged student body by providing a platform for students to explore, discuss, and analyze policy issues.

name: Sewing and Quilting Club @ Tech
summary: A gathering space for students who love sewing, quilting, embroidering, or crafting! We aim to provide a supportive, collaborative community for crafters and help teach students about the different types of fabric arts.

name: American Cancer Society @ GT
summary: The American Cancer Society Chapter at Georgia Tech is a student-led organization dedicated to eliminating cancer by initiating and supporting ACS programs and activities within the Georgia Tech community.

name: Dodgeball Club @ GT
summary: Dodgeball Club @ GT provides Georgia Tech students with a recreational opportunity to relax and destress, and to play competitive dodgeball,

name: International Council on Systems Engineering at Georgia Tech
summary: International Council on Systems Engineering at Georgia Tech promotes academic or extracurricular activities, organize outreach events, and provide networking opportunities to students interested in Systems Engineering.

name: Women+ in Biology
summary: Women+ in Biology at Georgia Tech is a student-led group that aims to connect all members of the biology community across career stages to foster belonging and career development through mentoring, support, networking, outreach and social events.

name: Linguistics at Georgia Tech
summary: Linguistics at Georgia Tech is a place for people interested in learning about linguistics and various languages to connect and access new opportunities.

name: Kurdish Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Kurdish Student Association at Georgia Tech is to support Kurdish students in adjusting to academic and social life at Georgia Tech while giving a sense of community and connection to their heritage.

name: Bioconnect @ Georgia Tech
summary: We foster and strengthen the biotechnology community within Georgia Tech and the greater Atlanta area by providing educational resources, networking opportunities, and awareness initiatives.

name: LibreTech Collective
summary: We work to promote, advocate for, and develop free and open-source: software, hardware, and knowledge through collaboration, education, and public engagement.

name: Bug Club @ Georgia Tech
summary: Cultivating the understanding of, conservation of, and appreciation of arthropods and various other invertebrate organisms commonly labeled as bugs through teaching, nature-walks, and conservation effort

name: Health and Business Alliance
summary: To bring together students with interests in both pre-health jobs as well as business. This could include those who want to own their own practice or those who want to manage a hospital.

name: Tamil Sangam at Georgia Tech
summary: Tamil Sangam is a cultural and community organization established to preserve, promote, and celebrate the Tamil language, culture, heritage, and traditions.

name: Scheller Pride
summary: The purpose of Scheller Pride is to foster a supportive, inclusive, and connected community for LGBTQIA+ graduate students and graduate student allies at the Scheller College of Business.

name: CanSat Robotics Club
summary: We develop small autonomous robots designed to operate in and from small rocket launches. Our primary goal is participation in the annual ARLISS CanSat Comeback Competition. https://www.aeropac.org/arliss/about/

name: CPIES Graduate Student Committee
summary: The CPIES Graduate Student Committee fosters collaboration among the Departments of Physics, Mathematics, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences, Biology, Chemistry and Biochemistry, and Psychology.

name: Scholars of Finance
summary: Scholars of Finance is a finance based volunteering and professional development club with a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

name: Grad Latin Club @ Georgia Tech
summary: The purpose of Grad Latin Club is to: 1) Develop, empower, and advance Latin business leaders and share the Latin culture with the Scheller MBA community 2) Offer networking opportunities for club members 3) Host talks and information sessions

name: SPARK Together
summary: The SPARK Together student organization seeks to build a vibrant community of students committed to compassionate leadership, personal growth, and meaningful service.

name: Project Hikmah
summary: Project Hikmah is a student-led journalism club at Georgia Tech. We empower students to research, create, and share stories with clarity and confidence, covering topics from medicine and physics to human rights through an Islamic-informed lens.

name: Disabilities in Business
summary: Disabilities in Business (DIB) exists to foster awareness, inclusion, and empowerment for people with disabilities and allies within the business community.

name: CEE Graduate Student Advisory Council
summary: The purpose of CEE GSAC is to facilitate community building and to serve as a liaison between the School of Civil and Environmental Engineering (CEE) and its graduate students.

name: ENGin at Georgia Tech
summary: ENGin at Georgia Tech is a chapter of the ENGin program, a nonprofit organization advancing social and economic growth in Ukraine. The program pairs English-speaking volunteers with Ukrainians students interested in learning and practicing English.

name: Etch-A-Tech
summary: We are a casual sketching club where we host workshops on media and style explorations.

name: Pre-Optometry at Tech
summary: The preoptometry club at Georgia tech serves to bring together students interested in pursuing optometry as well as foster more interest in the field.

name: Stand-Up Club at Georgia Tech
summary: We provide a venue for students interested in stand-up comedy to write, share, perform, and review comedic material at Georgia Tech.

name: The Helping Hive @ GT
summary: The Helping Hive is a student-led organization at Georgia Tech dedicated to providing free, one-on-one virtual academic support to K-12 students from underserved, immigrant, and refugee backgrounds.

name: SEWB Celebration and Community Building Committee
summary: SEWB Celebration and Community Building Committee

name: Brook Byers Institute for Sustainable Systems
summary: Brook Byers Institute for Sustainable Systems

name: TechMade
summary: TechMade

name: ECE Graduate Student Organization
summary: The purpose of the ECE Graduate Student Organization (GSO) is to serve as the primary liaison between ECE faculty and graduate students while building a stronger, more connected graduate community within the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

name: Substance Safety Initiative
summary: We aim to educate Georgia Tech students and staff about drug addiction and its effects on the addict and people around them.
